questions.question: What is the narrator's primary reason for feeling resentment toward Jerry's mother?
options: Because she was living in poverty
Because she sent him skates instead of gloves
Because she left a son of such high integrity in an orphanage
Because she did not have a job in Mannville
questions.answer: Because she left a son of such high integrity in an orphanage
questions.explanation: The narrator finds it difficult to understand how a mother could stay away from a child like Jerry, regardless of her financial situation.

questions.question: Jerry tells the narrator that he plans to buy his mother a pair of ___ with the dollar he earned.
questions.explanation: Jerry specifically asks if a dollar is enough to buy white gloves, which he says his mother likes.

questions.question: Complete the sentence regarding Jerry's work ethic.
questions.text: Jerry was a ___ agent who took ___ for his work without making excuses.
questions.explanation: The text describes him as a 'free-will agent' who took responsibility even when things went wrong.
These pairings match the specific objects and characters to their descriptions in the final parts of the story.

questions.question: How did Jerry respond when the narrator announced they were leaving tomorrow?
options: He asked for the narrator's address
He did not answer and walked away in silence
He cried and hugged the dog
He asked for one last payment
questions.answer: He did not answer and walked away in silence
questions.explanation: The narrator observes that Jerry did not speak and simply watched him walk away up the hill.

questions.question: According to Jerry, in what town does his mother currently live?
questions.explanation: Jerry mentions several times that his mother lives in Mannville.

questions.question: Complete the description of the items Jerry brought to the narrator.
questions.text: Jerry brought back ___ maple leaves and ___ boughs.
questions.explanation: The narrator describes the gifts from the woods as vermilion maple leaves and chestnut boughs.

questions.question: What does the narrator mean by saying Jerry's courtesy was 'Instinctive'?
options: He was trained well by the orphanage
It was a natural part of his character that required no formal words
He only acted polite when he was being watched
He was trying to impress the narrator for gifts
questions.answer: It was a natural part of his character that required no formal words
questions.explanation: The text notes that Jerry might not have even had use for the phrase 'thank you' because his kindness was so natural.

questions.question: When the narrator was delayed by fog, Jerry gave Pat some of his own ___.
questions.explanation: Jerry told the narrator that because no one could drive in the fog, he brought Pat his own breakfast.

pairs.right: Payment for caring for the dog

questions.explanation: These matches identify the specific items mentioned in Jerry's conversation about his mother's gifts.
